246-- Better performance for hundreds of labeled PM blocks

131-### 6.3.1 Zoom Levels and Data Representation
132-
133-| Zoom Level | Time Span | Granularity | Visual Representation |
134-|------------|-----------|-------------|----------------------|
135-| Strategic | 12 years | Yearly | Heatmap cells (PM density) |
136:| Tactical-Wide | 1 year | Monthly | Compressed Gantt blocks |
137-| Tactical | 12 weeks | Weekly | Full Gantt blocks with labels |
138-| Operational | 4 weeks | Daily | Detailed cards with all info |
139-| Detail | 1 week | Daily | Expanded cards with crew assignment |
140-
141-### 6.3.2 Strategic View (Heatmap)
--
226-**CRITICAL:** The canvas uses **hybrid rendering** to optimize for each zoom level:
227-
228-| Zoom Level | Renderer | Rationale |
229-|------------|----------|-----------|
230-| Strategic (0-4) | **deck.gl (WebGL)** | Heatmap cells, no text needed |
231:| Tactical-Wide (4-8) | **deck.gl (WebGL)** | Compressed blocks, minimal text |
232-| Tactical (8-12) | **Konva (HTML5 Canvas)** | Gantt bars with crisp text labels |
233-| Operational (12-16) | **Konva (HTML5 Canvas)** | Detailed cards with readable text |
234-| Detail (16-20) | **React DOM** | Rich interactions, form inputs |
235-
236-**Why Hybrid?**

   223тЖТ
   224тЖТ### 6.3.5 Hybrid Rendering Architecture
   225тЖТ
   226тЖТ**CRITICAL:** The canvas uses **hybrid rendering** to optimize for each zoom level:
   227тЖТ
   228тЖТ| Zoom Level | Renderer | Rationale |
   229тЖТ|------------|----------|-----------|
   230тЖТ| Strategic (0-4) | **deck.gl (WebGL)** | Heatmap cells, no text needed |
   231тЖТ| Tactical-Wide (4-8) | **deck.gl (WebGL)** | Compressed blocks, minimal text |
   232тЖТ| Tactical (8-12) | **Konva (HTML5 Canvas)** | Gantt bars with crisp text labels |
   233тЖТ| Operational (12-16) | **Konva (HTML5 Canvas)** | Detailed cards with readable text |
   234тЖТ| Detail (16-20) | **React DOM** | Rich interactions, form inputs |
   235тЖТ
   236тЖТ**Why Hybrid?**
   237тЖТ
   238тЖТdeck.gl uses WebGL for rendering, which has limitations for text-heavy views:
   239тЖТ- Font atlases blur during zoom operations
   240тЖТ- Hit-testing for drag-and-drop is complex in WebGL
   241тЖТ- Text rendering feels less crisp than native canvas/DOM
   242тЖТ
   243тЖТBy switching to Konva (HTML5 Canvas 2D) at tactical zoom levels, we get:
   244тЖТ- Crisp text rendering at all scales
   245тЖТ- Native canvas hit-testing for smooth drag-and-drop
   246тЖТ- Better performance for hundreds of labeled PM blocks
   247тЖТ
   248тЖТ```typescript
   249тЖТinterface CanvasLayers {
   250тЖТ  // === DECK.GL LAYERS (Strategic/Overview) ===
   251тЖТ  // Used for zoom levels 0-8 where text is minimal
   252тЖТ  
   253тЖТ  strategicHeatmapLayer: HeatmapLayer;    // PM density heatmap
   254тЖТ  timelineAxisLayer: LineLayer;            // Time axis lines
   255тЖТ  quarterBoundaryLayer: LineLayer;         // Quarter separators
   256тЖТ  
   257тЖТ  // === KONVA LAYERS (Tactical/Operational) ===
   258тЖТ  // Used for zoom levels 8-16 where text labels are critical
   259тЖТ  
   260тЖТ  ganttBlockLayer: Konva.Layer;            // PM blocks with labels
   261тЖТ  constraintWallLayer: Konva.Layer;        // Compliance windows
   262тЖТ  trainLabelLayer: Konva.Layer;            // Train number labels
   263тЖТ  tooltipLayer: Konva.Layer;               // Hover tooltips
   264тЖТ  dragPreviewLayer: Konva.Layer;           // Drag-and-drop preview
   265тЖТ  
   266тЖТ  // === REACT DOM OVERLAY (Detail) ===
   267тЖТ  // Used for zoom level 16+ where rich interactions needed
   268тЖТ  
   269тЖТ  detailCardOverlay: React.ReactNode;      // Expanded PM cards
   270тЖТ  editFormOverlay: React.ReactNode;        // Inline editing
   271тЖТ}
   272тЖТ
   273тЖТ// Layer visibility by zoom level
   274тЖТfunction getActiveRenderer(zoom: number): 'deckgl' | 'konva' | 'react' {
   275тЖТ  if (zoom < 8) return 'deckgl';
   276тЖТ  if (zoom < 16) return 'konva';
   277тЖТ  return 'react';
   278тЖТ}
   279тЖТ```
   280тЖТ
   281тЖТ**Transition Handling:**
   282тЖТ
   283тЖТWhen crossing renderer boundaries, animate opacity to avoid jarring switches:
   284тЖТ
   285тЖТ```typescript
   286тЖТfunction handleZoomChange(prevZoom: number, newZoom: number): void {
   287тЖТ  const prevRenderer = getActiveRenderer(prevZoom);
   288тЖТ  const newRenderer = getActiveRenderer(newZoom);
   289тЖТ  
   290тЖТ  if (prevRenderer !== newRenderer) {
   291тЖТ    // Cross-fade over 200ms
   292тЖТ    animateOpacity(prevRenderer, 1, 0, 200);
   293тЖТ    animateOpacity(newRenderer, 0, 1, 200);
   294тЖТ  }
   295тЖТ}
   296тЖТ```

content.text:
Based on my thorough analysis of the specification and existing code, I can now provide a comprehensive report on the Tactical-Wide view requirements.

## Tactical-Wide View Requirements (Spec 006-user-interface.md)

### 1. Tactical-Wide View Overview
The Tactical-Wide view is the **intermediate zoom level** between Strategic and Tactical views in the MMS UI infinite canvas.

### 2. Zoom Level Coverage
- **Zoom range:** 4-8 (between Strategic 0-4 and Tactical 8-12)
- **Zoom level name:** `TACTICAL_WIDE`
- **Renderer:** deck.gl (WebGL) - same as Strategic view
- **Threshold configuration:**
  ```typescript
  TACTICAL_WIDE: { min: 4, max: 8, renderer: 'deckgl' }
  ```

### 3. Time Granularity
- **Time span:** 1 year visible
- **Granularity:** Monthly (12 months per year)
- **Visual representation:** "Compressed Gantt blocks"

### 4. Data Requirements

The spec does NOT provide explicit data types for Tactical-Wide, but based on the zoom level table and hybrid rendering architecture, it should display:

**Inferred requirements:**
- Monthly aggregated PM blocks (compressed from weekly/daily detail)
- Each block represents PMs scheduled within that month
- Minimal text labels (per hybrid rendering rationale: "Compressed blocks, minimal text")
- Status-based color coding (inherited from PM status colors)
- Train rows (Y-axis)
- Monthly columns (X-axis)

**Data structure (inferred from existing patterns):**
Since Tactical-Wide uses deck.gl like Strategic, it would need:
- Monthly PM aggregates per train
- PM count per month
- Block positioning data
- Status/color information
- Possibly PM type indicators (C1, C2, C3, etc.)

### 5. Visual Requirements

**From spec Section 6.3.1:**
- Visual Representation: "Compressed Gantt blocks"

**From spec Section 6.3.5 (Hybrid Rendering):**
- Renderer: deck.gl (WebGL)
- Rationale: "Compressed blocks, minimal text"
- Uses zoom levels 0-8 where "text is minimal"

**Inferred visual characteristics:**
- Blocks should be rectangles representing PM activities in each month
- Compressed = simplified compared to full Gantt (no detailed labels like track assignments)
- Color-coded by PM status (scheduled, in progress, completed, etc.)
- Minimal or no text labels on blocks themselves
- Possibly just PM type codes (C1, C2, C3, C4) if space permits
- Should support hover tooltips for details

### 6. Implementation Notes

**Key differences from Strategic view:**
- Strategic shows yearly PM density heatmaps (intensity-based colors)
- Tactical-Wide shows monthly Gantt blocks (discrete PM blocks with status colors)

**Key differences from Tactical view:**
- Tactical uses Konva for crisp text rendering with full labels
- Tactical-Wide uses deck.gl for performance with minimal text
- Tactical shows weekly granularity with detailed info (track, hours, etc.)
- Tactical-Wide shows monthly granularity with compressed blocks

**Missing from spec:**
The specification **does not include** a dedicated section (6.3.2.5 or similar) with a visual mockup for Tactical-Wide view, unlike Strategic (6.3.2) and Tactical (6.3.3) views. This suggests implementation details should follow the patterns established by:
1. Strategic heatmap rendering approach (deck.gl layers)
2. Tactical Gantt block visual design (but compressed)
3. Monthly time scale (12 months visible)

### 7. File Paths for Implementation

**Existing implementations:**
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system/src/components/canvas/layers/types.ts` - Type definitions
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system/src/components/canvas/layers/StrategicHeatmapLayer.tsx` - Strategic view (deck.gl)
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system/src/components/canvas/layers/TacticalGanttLayer.tsx` - Tactical view (Konva)

**Expected new file:**
A new deck.gl layer component should be created, likely named:
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system/src/components/canvas/layers/TacticalWideLayer.tsx`

This layer would bridge the gap between yearly heatmaps and weekly Gantt charts with monthly compressed blocks.
